zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton於1210年出生在英國爾武特金頓時，他的父親Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest25歲，母親Joan de Kingsley26歲。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1258年在法國加斯可享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton于1210年出生在英国爾武特金頓时，他的父亲Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest25岁，母亲Joan de Kingsley26岁。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1258年在法国加斯可享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Donne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Dunn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn ‘descendant of Donn’, a byname meaning ‘brown-haired’ or ‘chieftain’. Compare Dunne . This name has sometimes been Anglicized as Brown .
English: nickname for a dark complexioned or swarthy man, from Middle English dun ‘dun, dark’ (Old English dunn ‘dull brown’).
Scottish: habitational name from Dun in Angus, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’. Compare Dun .

variants.name: Done
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Alfredo, Angel, Berto, Cristobal, Dinora, Eloisa, Francisco, Generoso, Gustavo, Juan.
English: variant of Donne, itself a variant of Dunn .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton was born in 1210, in Utkinton, Cheshire, England, his father, Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest, was 25 and his mother, Joan de Kingsley, was 26. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1258, in Gascogne, France, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton est né en 1210, à Utkinton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Joan de Kingsley, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1258, à Gascogne, France, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton 1210 in Utkinton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest 25 und seine Mutter Joan de Kingsley 26. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1258 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Gascogne, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton è nato il nel 1210 nel Utkinton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Joan de Kingsley, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1258, nel Gascogne, Francia, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Donne of Utkintonが1210年にイギリスチェシュアイアウトキントンで生まれた時，父Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forestは25歳，母Joan de Kingsleyは26歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1258年にフランスガスコグンで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton(이)가 1210년 영국 체실르 우트킨턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Kingsley(은)는 26세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1258년 프랑스 가스코그니에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton nasceu em 1210, em Utkinton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Kingsley, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1258, em Gascogne, França, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton родился в 1210 г. в местности уткинтон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Kingsley, 26. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1258 г. в местности гаскогн, Франция в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Donne of Utkinton nació en 1210, en Utkinton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ranulph Donne King's Master of the Delemere Forest, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Joan de Kingsley, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1258, en Gascogne, Francia, a la edad de 48 años.
